signals.raw:
Most Recent Confirmed Data (April 2, 2026):**

South Korea's **annual inflation rose to 2.2% in March 2026**, up from 2.0% in February but below the 2.4% forecast.[2] The monthly increase was 0.3%, weaker than the 0.6% expected.[2]

signals.headline: Key Context for Prediction Markets:**
signals.raw:
Key Context for Prediction Markets:**

The Bank of Korea held its policy rate steady at 2.5% and warned that **upside inflation risks dominate**, citing surging oil prices (past $100/barrel from the Iran conflict).[2] The central bank raised its full-year inflation outlook to 2.2% from 2.1%, and economists warned inflation could exceed 2.5% if oil prices remain elevated.[1][2]
